items.title: BofA cuts India's Nifty 50 earnings forecast as stagflation fears rise
items.summary: BofA Securities has significantly lowered its earnings growth forecast for Nifty 50 companies to 8.5% for FY27, citing rising stagflation risks and potential impact of high crude oil prices. The brokerage also reduced India's GDP growth estimate and warned of a severe downturn in a worst-case scenario. Despite this, a resolution to the Iran conflict could boost the market.

items.title: D-St eyes ‘Sell on Rise’ strategy amid West Asia tensions
items.summary: Indian markets brace for volatility as the West Asia conflict fuels investor nervousness. Despite oversold conditions, a bearish undertone persists, with Nifty and Sensex facing their sixth consecutive weekly decline. The RBI's upcoming monetary policy review will be closely watched for sentiment cues. Analysts suggest cautious trading with specific strategies for Nifty and recommend select stocks for potential gains.

items.title: RBI may keep rates unchanged, focus on rupee stability and bond yields
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to maintain interest rates and its policy stance unchanged due to global economic uncertainties, including the US-Israel conflict impacting energy prices and fiscal deficit concerns. Policymakers will closely monitor the rupee's performance and potential capital outflows, with the RBI possibly considering measures to support the currency without an aggressive immediate response.

items.title: FY26 IPO performance: Only 1 in 3 delivered returns amid market volatility
items.summary: The primary equity market in FY26 presented a challenging landscape, with most IPOs failing to deliver returns post-listing due to heightened volatility and foreign investor exodus. While only a third of the 109 mainboard IPOs posted positive returns, select companies like Ather Energy and Belrise Industries achieved significant gains.

items.title: Wingstop: Antonio Swad. A Brilliant Idea — And a Nail-Biting Exit
items.summary: A lot of founders spend their lives chasing one big idea. Antonio Swad had two. The first? Migrating chicken wings from the Happy Hour buffet to the center of the plate. The second? Building a pizza business that catered to a very specific demographic: Latinos. That first idea became Wingstop, a deep-fried wing concept that grew to 3,000 stores. The second became Pizza Patron, a franchise that rewarded customers for ordering in Spanish, and let them pay in pesos. This is the story of how Antonio
items.source_name: How I Built This with Guy Raz

items.title: RBL Bank shares jump 4% after exceptional Q4 update, RBI’s approval for Emirates NBD’s 74% stake acquisition
items.summary: RBL Bank shares rose sharply on Monday after strong Q4 business numbers and the RBI’s nod for Emirates NBD to acquire up to 74% stake in the bank. Robust deposits and loan growth boosted sentiment, with the lender crossing Rs 2.5 lakh crore in total business. Analysts remain positive amid exceptional momentum.

items.title: Wipro shares gain 3% after bagging Olam deal worth more than $1 billion
items.summary: Wipro Limited share price surged 3% following a significant deal with Olam Group. This multi-year strategic transformation contract is valued at over one billion dollars. Wipro will also acquire Olam's IT division, Mindsprint. The partnership aims to enhance Olam's operations across its entire value chain. This engagement marks a major expansion for Wipro in the food and agri-business sector.

items.title: As 401(k) balances swell, financial advisors warn of retirement planning pitfalls
items.summary: While a big retirement account balance is a good problem to have, it can come with challenges, financial advisors say.

items.title: There’s A War Of Independents In Podcasting
items.summary: Podcasting was developed by small, niche, independent creators. While corporate podcast networks and celebrities have brought more attention to the industry, they've also endangered those indie entrepreneurs.

items.title: While other CEOs freeze entry-level roles, this AI founder is hiring Gen Z with zero experience
items.summary: 38% of bosses plan to hire fewer grads this year. This ex-Google exec is doing the opposite—hiring Gen Z with zero experience or degree required

items.title: Analyzing The Statistical Prevalence Of Lawyers Getting Snagged By AI Hallucinations In Their Court Filings
items.summary: Attorneys are in trouble for including AI-hallucinated legal citations in their court filings. How prevalent is this? I provide a numeric analysis. An AI Insider scoop.

items.title: AI data center boom ‘stress tests’ insurers as private capital floods in
items.summary: Rapid technological advancements and the huge sums of money flowing into the data center are posing both risks and rewards for insurers.

items.title: Gold below Rs 1.5 lakh, silver down Rs 2,000 on dollar strength, Trump’s power plant threat to Iran. What should investors do?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices dipped on Monday amid a stronger dollar and escalating Middle East tensions, dampening hopes for U.S. Federal Reserve rate cuts. Traders are now largely discounting any rate reductions this year. Experts advise caution, suggesting profit-booking on rallies and waiting for dips to initiate fresh positions.

items.title: Further Ado Caps A Big Stakes Day For Brad Cox At Keeneland
items.summary: On perhaps the biggest day of the Spring Meet at Keeneland, trainer Brad Cox won three graded Stakes races including the $1.25 million Blue Grass Stakes.

items.title: A Seabourn Seder: Luxury And Tradition At Sea
items.summary: Jewish travelers on the luxury cruise ship, Seabourn Encore, experience feelings of connection through a shared Seder meal.
